codeReview.summary: I reviewed the prior failed review at `.factory/validation/net-http-runtime/scrutiny/reviews/http-crud-server-startup-and-request-surface.json`, the original feature commit `ec0c4cb81eb6e3b16084a7653a09a7dd8a879145`, the fix feature metadata/handoff/transcript skeleton for session `6220d653-2bb9-4b4e-b784-198b20517c3d`, the `compiler-worker` skill, and the shared-state docs. The original blocking issue is addressed: `typecheck_exn` now predeclares module-level `ItemLet` bindings before typechecking functions (`lib/typecheck.ml:2419-2425`), so functions no longer depend on source order to see file-scope globals. The fix also adds direct regression coverage for both declaration orders in typecheck tests (`test/test_typecheck.ml:1255-1260`) and for reordered codegen/HTTP fixtures (`test/test_codegen.ml:3241-3246`). I did not find a new correctness issue in the fix diff.

summary: Pass. The fix closes the prior blocking gap by predeclaring module-level lets before function typechecking, and the added regression tests cover both basic and HTTP-oriented reordered declaration cases without introducing a new review finding.
